title: Can a Fractional CMO Handle Paid Advertising Strategy? | Daniel Fox
meta_description: Yes, and most paid problems are not paid problems. Your ads manager owns the dashboard; nobody owns whether the message fits the customer. Where paid really stalls. Daniel Fox.

mainEntity.name: Can a fractional CMO handle paid advertising strategy?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, and the first thing worth saying is that most paid problems are not paid problems. Your ads manager owns the dashboard, the bids, and the budgets. Almost nobody owns whether the message in those ads actually fits the customer. When paid stalls, that gap is usually where it is hiding, not in the media buying. A fractional CMO audits the message before the media settings, hunts the borrowed clichés that never fit this company, and re-derives the positioning from the buyer's values. Daniel Fox is a fractional B2C CMO who does that work above the ads manager.

@graph.headline: Can a fractional CMO handle paid advertising strategy?
@graph.description: Most paid problems are not paid problems. Why declining performance usually lives in the message, not the media buying, and what a fractional CMO does above the ads manager.
